source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
A square of area $125 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$ was divided into five parts of equal area - four squares and one L-shaped figure as shown in the picture. Find the length of the shortest side of the L-shaped figure.
<image1>

solving depends on the geometric partition and side relationships visible in the figure

requires relating areas of four square subparts to dimensions of the L-shaped region
the visual arrangement introduces geometry beyond a single direct calculation
multiple-choice answer is an exact radical expression, suggesting algebraic derivation rather than a one-step lookup
verifiability: answer_type=short_text with a specific exact expression
multiple-choice options are provided, enabling direct answer checking
the problem is a deterministic geometry question with a unique numeric/algebraic target

decision_reasons: question, answer, and required image are all present with registry_passed=true

no asset issues are listed in the registry record
